tasks.name: Documentation refresh
tasks.description: This description is intentionally long so the table renderer has to wrap it across multiple lines while keeping the row readable and aligned with the task hierarchy.

children.name: README rewrite
children.description: Normal prose wraps at word boundaries. The row grows taller so the full sentence stays visible inside the Description column.

children.name: CLI examples
children.description: Single long tokens are not split automatically, so this intentionally ridiculous identifier shows the edge case: SuperLongUnbrokenTokenThatWillNotWordWrapEvenThoughEverythingElseDoes

children.name: Docs sign-off
children.description: Milestone descriptions wrap the same way as task descriptions in table mode.

tasks.name: Release preparation
tasks.description: A second phase makes the table more realistic and shows that wrapped rows can coexist with short rows without breaking the grid.

children.name: Announcement draft
children.description: Another longer paragraph that wraps naturally and demonstrates the intended behavior for human-written descriptions.
